title: dactylorhiza × grandis nothosubsp. grandis
intro: Dactylorhiza × grandis nothosubsp. grandis is a hybrid orchid species, resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza fuchsii and Dactylorhiza lapponica, commonly known as the fleshy or northern marsh orchid. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, derived from its bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Dactylorhiza × grandis nothosubsp. grandis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory conditions, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Dactylorhiza × grandis nothosubsp. grandis is typically used as a dried root or extract, often combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, or capsules.
